prompt: I have a bubbletea v2 app and I want to add a confirmation modal. When the user presses 'd' on a list item, a small centered dialog should pop up that says 'Delete this note? (y/n)'. While the modal is open, the list behind it should still be visible but the keyboard input should only go to the modal. When they press y it should delete and close, n or esc should cancel. How do I structure this with my existing state machine approach (using uint const iota for states)?
assertions: Adds a confirmModal (or similar) state to the existing state machine via iota
Uses lipgloss.Place() with lipgloss.Center alignment to center the modal
Modal has a border (lipgloss.RoundedBorder or similar)
The background list content is rendered underneath the modal (not blank)
Keyboard input in the confirmModal state only handles y/n/esc — other keys are not forwarded to the list
Pressing y triggers a delete action and returns to listView
Pressing n or esc cancels and returns to listView
